shortDescription.text: Confirms the observed behavior is part of legitimate functionality.

fullDescription.text: This rule identifies and confirms that the analyzed skill's behavior, such as parsing arguments, routing internal requests, and spawning subagents, aligns with common and legitimate patterns in AI agent frameworks. External threat intelligence has corroborated its benign intent, indicating no malicious activities like data exfiltration, arbitrary code execution, or unauthorized access.

message.text: Threat Intel CONFIRMED the legitimate nature of this skill. No specific lines were identified as suspicious in the initial analysis. The 'route' skill is designed for manual model selection (Haiku, Sonnet, Opus) and subagent spawning (e.g., 'fast-executor'). This is a legitimate and common pattern in AI agent frameworks, allowing users to override default behaviors. No confirmed malicious intent.
